items.title: Warn-then-fail ratchet on retrieval quality metric
items.description: Consumed by na-eng: added scripts/check-retrieval-quality-ratchet.sh and wired it into scripts/pre-push-gate.sh. The ratchet runs `ao retrieval-bench --search-eval` against .agents/rpi/ao-sessions-eval-queries-v1.json, reports any_relevant_at_k, avg_precision_at_k, hit counts, missing labels, and indexed turn count, warns below threshold before 500 indexed turns, and fails once strict mode is active. Tests cover pass, warn-only, strict-fail, and pre-push non-blocking warning behavior.

items.title: Upstream the bd hook shim fix (singular -> plural)
items.description: Repo .git/hooks/{post-checkout,post-merge,pre-commit} call `exec bd hook <event>` but installed bd only has `bd hooks` subcommand (plural). Worktree creation fails with `unknown command "hook"`. Patched locally via sed but .git/hooks/ is not versioned — fresh clones hit the bug. Either: (a) move shims to a tracked location like .githooks/ + core.hooksPath; (b) update bd to support legacy `bd hook`; (c) re-run `bd hooks install` to regenerate shims with current syntax. Encountered during 2026-05-01 swarm; 4 of 5 worker worktrees failed creation until fixed.

items.title: Crash-between-writes in event-sourced systems: 'do A; then write ledger event B' is unsafe under crash. On restart, A may have happened but B may not — duplicate work on next tick. Mitigation: deterministic dedup key on A's submission so retry collapses (idempotent submit), AND/OR write B FIRST with submission_id reservation so retry skips.

items.title: Operator-controlled YAML schedules without parser-level minimum-period enforcement enable DoS via crafted cron. A single typo like '* * * * *' (every minute) or '* * * * * *' (every second with 6-field parser) can flood the queue. Multi-tenant repos amplify the risk.

items.title: Skill SKILL.md files are prompts, not Go libraries. CLI subcommands (ao forge, ao harvest, ao compile) may be Cobra commands wrapping shell-script invocations or LLM calls — NOT importable Go APIs. Plans that say 'in-process executor calls existing skill packages' must verify the Go-level public API surface exists, OR rescope to invoke the CLI subcommand via in-process command execution.

items.title: Wave 0 verification gates have asymmetric ROI for multi-package-dependency plans. A 5-minute inline ls+grep verification can save ~1k LOC of unnecessary package extraction by surfacing absent/mismatched dependencies BEFORE Wave 1 dispatches workers. If 50%+ of cited packages are absent or surface-mismatched, rescope NOW (cheap) vs let workers discover during execution (expensive).

items.title: In zsh, parameter modifiers like ${VAR:r} (root pathname) and ${VAR:h} (head pathname) are interpreted INSIDE double-quoted variable expansions.
